_title: NFPA 70 Article 220 — Branch-Circuit, Feeder, and Service Load Calculations

articles.article_title: Branch-Circuit, Feeder, and Service Load Calculations
articles.scope: Load calculation methods — Standard (220.40) and Optional (220.82 dwelling / 220.84 multi-family / 220.86 office / 220.87 existing). Lighting load (220.12), receptacle load (220.14), motor load (220.50), AC load (220.82(C)).

key_sections.title: Lighting Load for Listed Occupancies
key_sections.summary: VA/ft² by occupancy from Table 220.12 (dwelling 3 VA/ft², office 3.5, school 3, store 3, hospital 2, etc.)

key_sections.title: Other Loads — All Occupancies
key_sections.summary: Receptacle outlets: each commercial receptacle = 180 VA; dwelling small-appliance 1500 VA per circuit

key_sections.title: General — Computation of Loads (Standard Method)
key_sections.summary: Sum of all loads applied to feeder; demand factors per Table 220.42 (dwellings), 220.44 (receptacles in non-dwelling), 220.54 (electric dryers), 220.55 (cooking equipment)

key_sections.title: Dwelling Unit — Optional Calculation
key_sections.summary: Alternative dwelling calc: 100% of first 10 kVA + 40% of remainder for general loads; HVAC at 100% or AC at 100% (whichever is larger)

key_sections.title: Existing Dwelling Unit
key_sections.summary: Optional calc for adding load to existing service — based on 80% of existing actual demand load

key_sections.title: Multifamily Dwelling — Optional
key_sections.summary: Demand factor table from 220.84(C) — applies to # units in the multifamily

key_sections.title: Determining Existing Loads
key_sections.summary: Use of recorded peak demand from utility for existing building

Table_220.12_General_Lighting_Loads_by_O.title: Unit Loads for General Lighting (VA per ft²)

rows.occupancy: Dwelling units (incl. lighting, small-appliance 1500VA × 2 + laundry 1500VA included in 220.42)

Table_220.42_Demand_Factors_for_General_.title: Demand Factor — Dwelling Unit General Lighting

common_errors: Computing dwelling load with 220.40 (Standard) when 220.82 (Optional) gives a smaller, code-compliant answer — choose the lower of the two valid methods
Forgetting the two small-appliance circuits (1500 VA each) + laundry circuit (1500 VA) when computing dwelling lighting load
Using 220.82 Optional without including the HVAC 100% (or AC 100%, whichever is larger) per 220.82(C)
Applying 220.12 lighting density to a kitchen — kitchen lighting comes through the dwelling load (220.42 + small-appliance)
related_iec_60364: IEC 60364-1 Annex Z (Method for assessment of maximum demand)
articles.divergence_notes: NEC 220 has TWO permitted load calc methods per project type (Standard 220.40 and Optional 220.82+) — the designer picks the lower; IEC has no parallel structured method, instead relying on diversity factors per circuit type. NEC 220.12 by-occupancy lighting load density (VA/ft²) is unique to NEC; IEC sizes from actual specified lighting + diversity.
articles.usage_notes: Always compute BOTH the Standard (220.40) and Optional (220.82+) for dwelling projects and use the smaller. Document which method was used in the design narrative.
